Detailed Description

Define Documentation

#define SKL_ASSERT (  EX   ) 
 

Value:

((void) ((EX) ||                                    \
   (Skl_Do_Assert( (SKL_CST_STRING)SKL_MK_STRING(EX), \
                   (SKL_CST_STRING)__FILE__, (int)__LINE__, 0), 0)))
Replacement for assert(), calling Skl_Do_Assert()

Typedef Documentation

typedef void* SKL_ANY
 

generic pointer type

typedef unsigned char SKL_BYTE
 

byte type (8bits, unsigned)

typedef const char* SKL_CST_STRING
 

generic constant string type

typedef short SKL_INT16
 

type for storing exactly 16bits, signed

typedef int SKL_INT32
 

type for storing exactly 32bits, signed

typedef long long int SKL_INT64
 

type for storing exactly 64bits, signed

typedef signed char SKL_INT8
 

type for storing exactly 8bits, signed

typedef SKL_UINT32 SKL_SAFE_INT
 

generic integer type capable of safely storing 64bits or 32bits pointers (depending on the platform)

typedef char* SKL_STRING
 

generic string type

typedef unsigned short SKL_UINT16
 

type for storing exactly 16bits, unsigned

typedef unsigned int SKL_UINT32
 

type for storing exactly 32bits, unsigned

typedef unsigned long long int SKL_UINT64
 

type for storing exactly 64bits, unsigned

typedef unsigned char SKL_UINT8
 

type for storing exactly 8bits, unsigned

Function Documentation

void* operator new (  size_t  s,
SKL_MEM_I *  m
)  [inline]
 

Overloaded new-with-placement operator, using SKL_MEM_I.

See also:
SKL_MEM_I

void Skl_Do_Assert (  SKL_CST_STRING  Condition,
SKL_CST_STRING  File,
int  Line,
SKL_CST_STRING  Msg
) 
 

When NDEBUG is _not_ defined (debug mode, e.g.), a faulty SKL_ASSERT( condition ) will call this function. Its main purpose is for you to be able to put a breakpoint with a debugger (beside ensuring that no assert is violated, of course).

Generated on Mon Sep 29 06:45:10 2003 for Sklmp4 MPEG-4 library by 1.3.4
